title: rudbeckia serotina var. sericea
intro: Rudbeckia serotina var. sericea is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Black-eyed Susan, a flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and bright yellow flowers with dark centers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The root and leaves of Rudbeckia serotina var. sericea are used in herbal preparations, often in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, to promote digestive health, alleviate menstrual cramps, and support the immune system. By incorporating this plant into their herbal routines, individuals may experience benefits such as improved immune function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being.
